channel.title: Comments for page "The Hatbot Chronicles"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Hatbot Chronicles"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm always stoked when I find a corner of lolfoundation that I haven't seen before. +100

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the epitomy of lolfoundation. And that’s praiseworthy. It’s definitely a blast from the past, what with it being almost ten years since this was posted (ouch), but it’s a small tale in the corner of the site. I think it’s important to remember your roots. And it is really funny.
Man, remember zalgo? (Heh. Mann.)
It’s a bit of shameless fun. I can appreciate that.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Why do you even have a cricket bat in your office?" asked Dr. ███████.

"In case of 008 breakouts, of course," said Dr. Mann.

"Of course. Continue."

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aaaaaaaaaaaaagh. This tale is really good in terms of story, and I really love the whole nightmarish ZALGO deal. But then there’s all the lolfoundation/superquirky OCs (emergency self-performed heart surgery? Destroying priceless technology because of mustard?) that really just destroys all the momentum. It’s a shame, really.

It’s not something we do, but I feel like a rewrite of this would be so good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's piping the output to /dev/null, not the program itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man just reading this I could tell that this was around 10 years old. Come to the discussion and sure enough, 2009. I miss when 4chan was still just a small corner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uhm a quite picky computer programmer here. If you put something in /dev/null its basicly deleted and not ran as a deamon

item.title: Re: command prompts at the end?
item.content:encoded:
Scorpion451 has the gist of it, but I figured I can go into a bit more detail. I'm by no means a Linux expert, but I do have some experience working in Linux.

Someone/something is trying to run Hatbot using the command "sudo -bK -u root '/home/L4/A_Valence/bin/hatbot -d &>/dev/null'". This is a bit dense, but "sudo" is short for "superuser do". Certain things are only able to be done by privileged users (superusers). The '-bK' argument a) runs the command in the background and b) invalidates the user's cached credentials (I ripped that from the sudo manual, and admittedly I don't know enough about cached credentials to explain that further).

This command fails because Dr. Mann's account is not a superuser, so the nefarious terminal user leverages Dr. Valence's account (this is the "su A_Valence" command), then the root account (which is basically the most privileged account). The last bit, "/home/L4/A_Valence/bin/hatbot -d &>/dev/null" runs Hatbot and directs any output messages to /dev/null, which you can think of as a trash hole, for lack of better words. Any output you send there goes bye-bye, so this is a way of running Hatbot silently.

I hope this cleared some things up for you :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a thrilling tale. I loved every second of this. Great work, Mann.

+1

item.title: Re: command prompts at the end?
item.content:encoded: I'm not overly familiar with linux systems, but from what I understand those commands were creating a new admin-level account on Dr. Mann's computer named A_Valence, and then activating hatbot as a hidden background program within that userspace.

item.title: command prompts at the end?
item.content:encoded: could please someone experienced on the matter explain what these command prompts at the end are or what they do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Clearly, those layabouts at Valve stole the idea from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… didnt think of that at all, that's kind of a funny coincidence

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was super fun to read. I especially liked the weird text in there. My only question is… did anyone else for some odd reason remember the game-mode "Mann Vs Machine"?

item.title: Possible Computer Virus for Reality?
item.content:encoded:
I don't know about you, but what happened to Hatbot seemed to cause something near the equivalentcy of a Reality Virus. Either that, or this creature it talked about (Zalgo) was breaking down the walls of reality, speaking a computerized version of Black Speech, and trying to get it to "sing a song to end the world".

Stupid cultist. Always trying to end the world, and now through the Internet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was an earlier, simpler time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the fuck is this even. My god. WHY THE ZALGO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This ends on such a perfect note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I certainly had alot of fun reading that. It didn't feel like it was taking itself to seriously, which is why I like it as much as I do. Never heard of Zalgo, but it's not too late to start haha!

http://www.redbubble.com/people/generalilya/works/12355152-fuck-trees-i-climb-clouds-motherfucker?p=t-shirt&style=mens&body_color=navy&print_location=front

Just might get this shirt now…
+1!

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: Oh, god… Who let hatbot into the forum?

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: That little hypocrite! He cussed at me about summoning Zalgo.

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded: They're not the same entity. Hatbot just keeps trying to summon Zalgo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …At what point did Hatbot and Zalgo apparently become the same entity?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OH MY GOD THIS IS HORRIFYING

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-153 does not exist anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It greatly amuses me that even secretaries are armed. I love the foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fuck trees i climb clouds motherfucker

item.title: Re:
item.content:encoded:
It was based primarily on the fact that Hatbot keeps threatening to kill me. In England. Every 24 hours.

I'm not sure why it has decided to be my nemesis, but I felt it was time I fought back through some means.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well…. That was certainly… different.

But unfortunately, it wasn't the epic mass outbreak of ZALGOesque madness that I had been hoping for. Seems a bit more like a narrative about Mann really…
